item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image on this article comes from here, which has been released onto the public domain. As such, it is compliant with CC 3.0.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright, so there are two tales that star SCP-1434's effects, that could easily serve as wikiwalk destinations. The first is Law and Order and the second is The Man in Orange. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Since recovery, investigations into several public disturbances have recovered other SCP-1434 fragments of varying size. Unrest is believed to have subsided when a party or parties unknown removed the object from the municipality. The object's whereabouts between incidents is as yet unknown, though research is continuing. Any outbreaks of police violence linked to recently introduced legislation should be studied for signs of object influence by the appropriate Mobile Task Force.

To this:

Since recovery, investigations into several public disturbances have recovered other SCP-1434 fragments of varying size. Unrest is believed to have subsided when a party or parties unknown removed the object from the municipality. The object's whereabouts between incidents is as yet unknown, though research is continuing. Any outbreaks of police violence linked to recently introduced legislation should be studied for signs of object influence by the appropriate Mobile Task Force.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My guess is it's implied with the "they enforce it without protest", which is the main reason I dislike this SCP. Might be a case of "blanket compulsion" or just "everyone has the idiot ball because I said so" Either one would make me dislike it regardless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why, for the same reason they were using shrimp to clear snow of course! (They weren't, until they had to.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hold on, why were people drinking gasoline anyway? Is it part of the anomaly? If it is, it should be stated in the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A wonderful look at silly laws XD makes me wonder how difficult it would be to find the scp if it were lost in a giant city, or if it would only affect a suburbs local police since it targets small areas

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is good, a bit funny and potentially horrifying. +12321

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Case 001—12/18/1987: Montgomery County in Maryland

Aaaaahhhh! I live in Maryland and work in Montgomery County! That gave me more of a shock to read than the insert-your-name-here things that pop up sometimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Are you SURE this site is fiction?

lol, in all seriousness, it WOULD explain a lot if this was real. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The source of Prohibition, perhaps?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does it have anything to do with the species called

+ spoiler
- hide

Bulldozer shrimp?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh! Well, I'm glad he's back.

VEZAZ, I AM GLAD YOU ARE BACK

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was deleted due to the author's personal reasons, along with the rest of his articles. When he returned, he reposted the lot of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, why did this need to get reposted? I thought it had gone well positive the first time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice to see this back, but yeah, the shrimp joke is a little obscure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The joke is, that the cops suspect anyone seen near a snowy highway of somehow using shrimp to clean it, and intervene. By running them over with snow-plows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably local law enforcement ensuring that no one is using shrimp getting hit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the idea - the containment measures are ingenious in their brevity - but find myself terminally confused by Case 001; why should not using shrimp make the snowplows any less safe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I think they're a little bitter, but to each their own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Welcome back. I'm especially glad to see this one back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They use those to clear snow? Sweet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A shrimp is a small aquatic crustacean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He returns!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still like it.

And, on a side note, I'm delighted that my tale, Law and Order, is no longer orphaned like a sad, sad child!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
:D (I'm assuming this is Vezaz coming back, otherwise it'll go breaking my heart.)

Editing this in:

Montgomery County in Maryland outlaws the use of shrimp in clearing snow from highways. Six injured in collisions with state-operated snowplows.

I always wondered but never asked. What is a shrimp in the context of the above sentence? I spent about 45 seconds googling and didn't see anything that looked promising.
